findings: Verified as an American Goldfinch (Spinus tristis), breeding male: compact small-finch proportions, short conical bill, one head, one beak, two wings, two legs/feet, and one short notched tail are depicted correctly.

Plumage and field marks agree with the supplied reference photos and live sources: bright yellow body, black forehead/crown area, black wings with white markings, and white tail patches. The expanded-wing study is consistent with the species’ black-and-white flight pattern.

All visible text is accurate: common and scientific names, Fringillidae family, measurements, habitat, behavior, sex/seasonal plumage descriptions, and palette. The measurement ranges match All About Birds exactly; Audubon independently corroborates the same overall size, weight, wingspan, and seasonal field marks.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.
No material species, anatomy, or text errors found.

verification_sources.title: American Goldfinch Identification — All About Birds
